interpretation: P2RY13 encodes the P2Y purinoceptor 13, a receptor for adenosine diphosphate (ADP) that is coupled to G(i)-proteins and is implicated in hematopoiesis and immune responses. The retrieved evidence indicates that P2RY13 is among the key diagnostic markers identified in a study focused on steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head (SONFH), where it was linked to immune cell infiltration patterns associated with the disease. This suggests that P2RY13 may play a role in the pathophysiology of SONFH, particularly in the context of immune modulation following steroid treatment. The evidence is classified as Tier 2, indicating a moderate level of confidence due to its association with immune mechanisms relevant to SONFH. Therefore, P2RY13 has potential as a biomarker for early detection of SONFH, given its involvement in immune responses that may be altered in this condition.
relevance_summary: P2RY13 is implicated as a potential biomarker for early detection of steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head due to its association with immune cell infiltration.
citations: Identification of key biomarkers in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head and their correlation with immune infiltration by bioinformatics analysis. (2022). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35042504/

result.title: P2RY13 (Q9BPV8)
result.text:
Protein: P2Y purinoceptor 13
Function: Receptor for ADP. Coupled to G(i)-proteins. May play a role in hematopoiesis and the immune system

result.title: P2RY13 ↔ diverticular disease
result.text:
Disease: diverticular disease
Description: A complex disorder characterised by mucosal outpouchings (diverticulae) of the colonic wall which can become infected and inflamed leading to diverticulitis, perforation and bleeding.
Association score: 0.120

result.title: P2RY13 ↔ lung adenocarcinoma
result.text:
Disease: lung adenocarcinoma
Description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.
Association score: 0.083

result.title: P2RY13 ↔ ulcerative colitis
result.text:
Disease: ulcerative colitis
Description: An inflammatory bowel disease involving the mucosal surface of the large intestine and rectum. It may present with an acute or slow onset and follows an intermittent or continuous course. Signs and symptoms include abdominal pain, diarrhea, fever, weight loss, and intestinal hemorrhage.
Association score: 0.076

result.title: P2RY13 ↔ abdominal abscess
result.text:
Disease: abdominal abscess
Description: An abscess located in the abdominal cavity, i.e., the cavity between the diaphragm above and the pelvis below. (From Dorland, 27th ed)
Association score: 0.064

result.title: Identification of key biomarkers in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head and their correlation with immune infiltration by bioinformatics analysis.
result.text: This study aimed to identify key diagnostic markers and immune infiltration of (SONFH) by bioinformatics analysis. Related SONFH datasets were downloaded from the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) database. First, we identified the differentially expressed genes (DEGs) and performed the functional enrichment analysis. Then weighted correlation network analysis (WGCNA) and the MCODE plug-in in Cytoscape were used to identify the diagnostic markers of SONFH. Finally, CIBERSORT was used to analyze the immune infiltration between SONFH and healthy controls, and the correlation between infiltrating immune cells and diagnostic markers was analyzed. TYROBP, TLR2, P2RY13, TLR8, HCK, MNDA, and NCF2 may be key diagnostic markers of SONFH. Immune cell infiltration analysis revealed that Memory B cells and activated dendritic cells may be related to the SONFH process. Moreover, HCK was negatively correlated with CD8 T cells, and neutrophils were positively correlated with those key diagnostic markers. TYROBP, TLR2, P2RY13, TLR8, HCK, MNDA, and NCF2 may be used as diagnostic markers of SONFH, and immune-related mechanism of SONFH and the potential immunotherapy are worthy of further study.
